name: dmitry
title: dmitry
description:
Deepmagic Information Gathering Tool
DMitry is a UNIX/(GNU)Linux command line application written in C.
DMitry can find possible subdomains, email addresses, uptime information,
perform tcp port scan, whois lookups, and more.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dmitry because Deepmagic Information Gathering Tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dmitry as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dmitry [Options] host` as documented upstream for dmitry.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dmitry -w mydomain.com` as documented upstream for dmitry.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dmitry -winsepo sometextfile.txt mydomain.com` as documented upstream for dmitry.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dmitry -winsepo mydomain.com` as documented upstream for dmitry.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dmitry with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dmitry --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install dmitry. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dmitry -w mydomain.com`. Further invocations from upstream: `dmitry -winsepo sometextfile.txt mydomain.com`; `dmitry -winsepo mydomain.com`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Deepmagic Information Gathering Tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: DMitry - Deepmagic Information Gathering Tool.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install dmitry
Install with: sudo apt install dmitry
